title: これ、スタックしている？ • 現在タスクは thin-core を維持したまま drift 耐性と自動反 映を次段まで進めること として扱います。まず現行の lane 可 用性を current-run で確認し、6 本の active lane を立てて から実装に入ります。 • Spawned Feynman [reviewer] └ [phase:plan] [family:layering] [provider_hint:codex] Current task: continue Kernel hardening without increasing always-loaded con...
summary_text: **Findings (ordered by severity)** 1. **High: completion proof is not machine-checked for loaded runtime state (false-green risk).** Current test only validates `--dry-run` plist output, not the loaded LaunchAgent after bootstrap. Evidence: [test-kernel-task-completion-backup.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/agent-orchestration/tests/test-kernel-task-completion-backup.sh#L345) only greps `<integer>600</integer>` from dry-run output; no assertion for `launchctl print` interval/path/state. 2. **High: no deterministic acceptance threshold for overlap/noise (`lock_busy`) in proof standard.** Runtime script emits `BLOCKED lock_busy` with `75`, but there is no test/spec threshold like “0 events in N minutes” or “rate < X/hour.” Evidence: [run-kernel-task-completion-backup.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/agent-orchestration/scripts/local/run-kernel-task-completion-backup.sh#L208) emits `BLOCKED lock_busy`; no corresponding bounded-window assertion in current backup tests. 3. **Medium: interval input is unvalidated; malformed values can generate invalid plist/runtime breakage.** `--interval` is accepted as-is and injected into `<integer>...</integer>` with no numeric/range validation. Evidence: [bootstrap-kernel-task-completion-backup-agent.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/agent-orchestration/scripts/local/bootstrap-kernel-task-completion-backup-agent.sh#L47) and [bootstrap-kernel-task-completion-backup-agent.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/agent-orchestration/scripts/local/bootstrap-kernel-task-completion-backup-agent.sh#L84). 4. **Medium (workflow): multi-lane evidence is not part of completion proof contract.** Current proof checks runtime/backup behavior, but there is no explicit assertion artifact that preflight/implementation preserved required multi-lane execution shape. This is a Kernel contract gap, not a runtime script bug. **Residual risks** - Stale log lines from old paths can still pollute human-only proof unless proof window is explicitly post-bootstrap timestamp bounded. - `600s` is still a policy guess unless backed by observed runtime distribution and overlap SLO checks.
